summary:
The dried blood spot test in newborns screens mainly for metabolic disorders by measuring hemoglobin levels, aiding early detection of issues like sickle cell disease or thalassemia. This method provides a non-invasive way to assess overall hematological status from just one small sample collected via pricking an infant’s heel. ```plaintext
Dried blood spot tests in neonates primarily screen for metabolic disorders through measurement of hemoglobin levels, facilitating early diagnosis of conditions such as sickle cell disease or thalassemia. These simple samples provide crucial information about infants' hematologic health without invasive procedures.
```
